payload.description:
## Behavior

As a Plexus runtime agent, I want a documentation knowledge base that exposes structured metadata so I can discover and load relevant docs incrementally without paying full content cost upfront.

```gherkin
Feature: Frontmatter-aware documentation knowledge base

  Scenario: Listing docs returns metadata summaries instead of raw paths
    Given a documentation tree at documentation/agent containing markdown files with YAML frontmatter
    When an agent calls plexus.docs.list
    Then the response is a list of entries
    And each entry has id, title, summary, namespace, and tags fields populated from frontmatter

  Scenario: Getting a doc by namespaced id returns metadata and content
    Given a doc with id mcp.execute-tactus.overview exists in the knowledge base
    When an agent calls plexus.docs.get with key mcp.execute-tactus.overview
    Then the response contains the full markdown body
    And the response contains the parsed frontmatter metadata

  Scenario: Unsafe keys are rejected
    Given a documentation tree at documentation/agent
    When an agent calls plexus.docs.get with a key containing path traversal
    Then the call fails with a structured error
    And no file outside the documentation root is read

  Scenario: README files are excluded from the agent index
    Given a documentation tree where each namespace folder contains a human-readable README.md
    When an agent calls plexus.docs.list
    Then no README entries appear in the listing

  Scenario: Related docs enable progressive disclosure
    Given a doc whose frontmatter lists related ids
    When an agent calls plexus.docs.get for that doc
    Then the response includes the related ids
    And those ids resolve via plexus.docs.get without additional discovery
```
payload.title: Documentation knowledge base behaves with frontmatter-aware progressive disclosure
